title: Rail Fence Cipher Part 1: Pattern Recognition
body:
Rail Fence Ciphers hide a message by arranging letters in a zig-zag pattern across several "rails" (rows). Even without decoding a message fully, you can often tell how many rails were used by looking at how the letters are distributed and how the pattern might "zig-zag" if written out. 
In this lesson, you'll use these visual and structural cues to identify the most likely number of rails used to encode a message.
lesson: CRYPTO-D1A-9f5a2f70, CRYPTO-D1B-55c1a1de, CRYPTO-D4A-8b7f12c9, CRYPTO-D4B-74ad0c21, CRYPTO-D5A-90c3e4e2, CRYPTO-D5B-1f0a76bd
